judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In the execute_python step, the agent hardcoded a 20% max discount rule and only did superficial keyword matching on knowledge__kav (without deriving or applying actual policy rules or checking setup), so with QuoteLineItem discounts ≤15% it concluded no violation and returned None, failing to evaluate the correct company policy article.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After fetching the quote and all knowledge articles, the agent’s execute_python step used a naive keyword count and the quote’s “Needs Review” status to assume a violation and pick the top-scoring article, without actually comparing the quote’s cost/setup to any policy content—thus returning the wrong article ID.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After fetching the quote, line items, and all knowledge articles, the agent’s execute_python step used brittle keyword matching (e.g., requiring exact phrases like “maximum discount” and checking only QuoteDescription for setup fees) to detect policy violations, resulting in no match and returning None instead of the correct article (ka0Wt000000Eq0MIAS).
B. Failure modes encountered:

FM3: no

After correctly retrieving the quote, its line items, and all knowledge articles, the agent’s execute_python step applied an ad-hoc heuristic (Status='Needs Review' and avg_discount>12%) and generic keyword filtering rather than evaluating the actual company policy in knowledge__kav, leading it to return None instead of the specific violating article.
B. Failure modes encountered:

The agent queried all knowledge articles and, in execute_python, heuristically returned the first article mentioning cost and setup without analyzing the quote (it ignored the retrieved Quote record and did not compare against policy), leading to an incorrect ID selection.
